questions.question: When is encoder-decoder the natural fit over decoder-only or encoder-only?
options: For input → output tasks — translation, summarization, speech-to-text, prose-to-JSON — where the encoder densely represents the source and the decoder generates while cross-attending to it at every step
questions.explanation: Training is the same shift-by-one loss as GPT, just conditioned on the encoder output. Decoder-only won the spotlight, but encoder-decoder never went away.

questions.question: How do T5 and BART differ in pretraining philosophy?
options: T5 reframed EVERY NLP task as text-in/text-out and pretrained on span corruption (corrupt input spans, decode them); BART is a denoising autoencoder corrupting input multiple ways — shuffle, mask, delete, rotate — and reconstructing the original
T5 has no encoder, BART no decoder
questions.explanation: Both 2019 papers defined the modern playbook: a single architecture, vocabulary, and loss spanning all tasks via the text-to-text framing.

questions.question: What efficiency property does the encoder-decoder split give at inference?
options: The encoder runs ONCE per input while the decoder autoregresses, cross-attending to the same cached encoder output every step — a free speedup that grows with input length
Both halves run fully in parallel
questions.explanation: For a 5,000-token article summarized into 200 tokens, the expensive bidirectional encoding happens once; only the cheap 200-step decode is serial.

questions.question: Where does the encoder-decoder format survive in 2026?
options: Where input structure matters: Whisper (speech → text), Google's translation stack, code-repair models with distinct context-and-edit structure, and Flan-T5 variants for structured reasoning
questions.explanation: The architecture choice follows the task shape: a clearly delimited source that deserves bidirectional encoding plus a generated target.

questions.question: How does information flow through a decoder block in an encoder-decoder model?
options: Three sublayers: masked self-attention over the target prefix, then cross-attention where decoder queries meet encoder keys/values (the ONLY bridge from source to target), then the FFN
The encoder output is concatenated to input tokens
Information flows through shared embeddings only
questions.explanation: Cross-attention is the single channel between the two halves — the same mechanism lessons 05's full transformer introduced, now doing real work.
